meaning: vt.鍚堟媿(褰辩墖),鍚堜綔婕斿嚭(鎴忓墽)
examples: Since Japan would coproduce these weapons under license to U.S. companies, it would need permission from Washington to export them.
Sharp is "in talks" with Italian utility Enel SpA to locally coproduce thin-film solar cells whose lower costs offset their lower efficiency than what is used by consumers.
"Harry Potter" producer David Heyman has signed on to coproduce the project with KatzSmith.
